_general.purpose: Library entries, kiwix search, vault retrieval, unified knowledge index, and curation lifecycle. Pass 'project' to scope (vault/knowledge actions are cross-project).
_general.notes:
The vault_search and knowledge_search actions are cross-project by default — the vault and unified knowledge index live outside any single project's data. library_* actions are project-scoped: each project's library is independent.

Curation actions (curation_promote, curation_reject, curation_bulk_action) emit substrate events (CurationCandidatePromoted / CurationCandidateRejected) visible in AuditLedger.

ENVELOPE-LEVEL REQUIREMENT — rationale gate (bug 1436). Mutating actions on this surface require a top-level `rationale` field when called by an agent actor — sits next to `action`, `params`, `project` on the dispatch envelope, NOT inside `params`. The dispatcher rejects empty / whitespace / boilerplate / <6-char rationales with `error: "rationale_required"`. Actions on this surface that require rationale: library_add, library_update, library_retire. Read actions (vault_search, knowledge_search, library_get, library_find, kiwix_search, parse_context, resolve_references, curation_*, etc.) do NOT require rationale.

kiwix_search is the local-first lookup reflex: 371 GB of indexed offline DevDocs (Rust, Python, JS/TS, Postgres, Redis, SQLite, Node, MDN, etc.), the Rust Book, StackExchange snapshots, and Wikipedia. Reach for it BEFORE web search or guessing a function signature — authoritative, offline, faster.

curation_bulk_action.purpose: Apply promote/reject to all matching candidates. Filter must include at least one of {origin, unscored_only} to prevent whole-project nukes. Emits CurationCandidatePromoted / CurationCandidateRejected substrate events visible in AuditLedger.

params.name: filter
params.description: Match criteria for which candidates to act on. Must include at least one of {origin, unscored_only}.

params.name: action
params.description: promote or reject.

params.name: reason
params.description: Required when action=reject; supplies the rejection reason for every affected candidate.

params.name: dry_run
params.description: When true, return what would be done without mutating.

params.name: limit
params.description: Cap the number of candidates touched. Defaults to 100.

errors.message: curation_bulk_action requires a filter containing at least one of {origin, unscored_only} — prevents accidental whole-project nukes.

curation_list.purpose: List pending curation_candidates for the project (compact projection).

params.name: origin
params.description: Filter by candidate origin.

params.name: scored
params.description: Filter by whether the candidate has been scored.

params.name: limit
params.description: Result limit. Defaults to 50.

curation_promote.purpose: Promote a pending candidate to a knowledge_pointer. Emits a CurationCandidatePromoted substrate event visible in AuditLedger. Optional overrides refine metadata at promotion time.

params.name: id
params.description: The candidate id.

params.name: override_question
params.description: Override the candidate's question at promotion time.

params.name: override_invoke_when
params.description: Override the candidate's invoke_when at promotion time.

params.name: override_description
params.description: Override the candidate's description at promotion time.

curation_read.purpose: Return the full candidate body for one curation_candidate.

params.name: id
params.description: The candidate id.

curation_reject.purpose: Mark a candidate rejected. Emits a CurationCandidateRejected substrate event visible in AuditLedger. Reason is REQUIRED and non-empty.

params.name: id
params.description: The candidate id.

params.name: reason
params.description: Why the candidate is being rejected. REQUIRED and non-empty.

errors.message: curation_reject requires a non-empty reason; rejection without rationale is not accepted.

kiwix_fetch.purpose: Fetch a specific page from a Kiwix ZIM archive by path. Companion to kiwix_search — search finds the path, fetch returns the body.

params.name: zim_id
params.description: The versioned ZIM identifier.

params.name: path
params.description: The page path within the ZIM.

kiwix_list_books.purpose: Enumerate the available ZIM archives on the local Kiwix instance. Use this when you don't know which zim_id to query.

kiwix_search.purpose: Query the local Kiwix instance for matching pages in a chosen ZIM archive — 371 GB of offline DevDocs (Rust, Python, JS/TS, Postgres, Redis, SQLite, Node, MDN, etc.), the Rust Book, StackExchange snapshots, and Wikipedia. Reach for this BEFORE web search or guessing a function signature: authoritative, offline, faster.

params.name: zim_id
params.description: The versioned ZIM identifier (e.g. devdocs_en_rust_2026-04). Call kiwix_list_books to enumerate available ZIMs when you don't know which to query.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Search pattern.

knowledge_report_miss.purpose: When a followed pointer turns out to be wrong or stale, call this to increment negative_feedback_count and optionally set staleness_hint. Takes pointer_id from knowledge_search results.

params.name: pointer_id
params.description: The pointer_id from a knowledge_search result.

params.name: staleness_reason
params.description: Optional reason describing why the pointer was stale (used as staleness_hint).

knowledge_search.purpose: Unified FTS5+Qwen retrieval over all indexed knowledge sources (vault, kiwix, tasks, chains, library, bugs); returns ranked hits with id, source_type, source_ref, question, invoke_when, quality_score. Reach for this when picking up an unfamiliar problem area BEFORE web search or guessing — the index points to the most likely existing answer across every surface this server knows about.

params.name: query
params.description: Free-text search query.

params.name: top_k
params.description: Number of top results to return. Defaults to 5.

library_add.purpose: Add a new entry to the project's library catalogue. Mutates the library state.

envelope_requirements.reason: Dispatcher policy gate (action-manifests/dispatch-policy.toml). Lives at the call envelope level (next to action/params/project), NOT inside params. Rejected on empty / whitespace / boilerplate / <6-char rationales with error=rationale_required.

library_cross_reference.purpose: Create or list cross-reference edges between library entries — the relational view of the catalogue.

library_find.purpose: Free-text find across the project's library entries. Entry point for catalogue lookup when you don't know the slug.

params.name: query
params.description: Free-text search query.

library_get.purpose: Return one library entry by slug. The single-row read for the project's knowledge catalogue.

params.name: slug
params.description: Library entry slug.

library_list_active.purpose: List every active library entry in the project's catalogue. Retired entries are excluded; use a different enumeration if you want the full set.

library_list_dewey.purpose: Enumerate library entries grouped by Dewey code — the structural classification view of the project's catalogue.

library_list_sections.purpose: Enumerate library entries grouped by section — the section taxonomy view of the project's catalogue.

library_retire.purpose: Mark a library entry retired — removes it from library_list_active enumerations while preserving the row for history. Inverse of library_add for stale entries.

params.name: slug
params.description: The library entry's slug.

envelope_requirements.reason: Dispatcher policy gate (action-manifests/dispatch-policy.toml). Lives at the call envelope level (next to action/params/project), NOT inside params. Rejected on empty / whitespace / boilerplate / <6-char rationales with error=rationale_required.

library_update.purpose: Update an existing library entry's fields. Mutates the library state.

params.name: slug
params.description: The library entry's slug.

envelope_requirements.reason: Dispatcher policy gate (action-manifests/dispatch-policy.toml). Lives at the call envelope level (next to action/params/project), NOT inside params. Rejected on empty / whitespace / boilerplate / <6-char rationales with error=rationale_required.

memory_read.purpose: Return the materialized memory digest for a project — the owned memory-read path. Aggregates every user-kind entry (fanned out to all projects) plus the feedback/project/reference entries scoped to the project, as a bullet digest (memory_markdown) plus structured entries. The read side of the memory organ for a session-start context injection; pairs with the record action that writes entries to the vault.

params.name: project
params.description: Project slug to scope project/feedback/reference entries to; user-kind entries are always included.

parse_context.purpose: Canonical orienting call. Resolve every reference-shape token in a user message AND surface domain-conditional context (skills, memory entries, vault notes, kiwix hits, disciplines triggered by message shape) in one unified envelope. parse_context is the agent's first call on every user prompt (modulo the discernment skill's skip rules: short conversational acknowledgments, slash-commands, pure continuations). Replaces the prior pattern of per-shape direct calls (chain_find, task_search, vault_search, kiwix_search, the available-skills list, …) — one round-trip surfaces everything potentially relevant.

params.name: message_text
params.description: The user message (or any agent-facing text) to scan for unresolved references and domain-conditional context. Empty string returns an empty References list and zero resolver calls.

params.name: session_id
params.description: Session identifier for the filter-cache. Defaults to the span's trace ID when omitted (the substrate stamps it from the request context). Pass explicitly when calling outside an MCP session (smoke harness, scripts).

params.name: top_k_per_shape
params.description: Cap on candidates each resolver returns after dispatcher post-processing. Defaults to 5.

params.name: include_no_hits
params.description: When true, references that resolved to no_hit stay in the References list (with recommended_action = acknowledge_no_hit_and_ask) instead of collapsing into the NoHitTokens slice. Default false.

params.name: total_budget_ms
params.description: Dispatcher wall-clock cap for the whole call, in milliseconds. Defaults to 2000 (2s) today; budget rises to 4000 (4s) when the broader resolver set lands. When the budget trips, truncated_by_budget=true is set on the response envelope; resolvers not reached surface ErrTotalBudgetExceeded as partial_failures.

params.name: cache_policy_override
params.description: Force a cache policy for this call. Accepted values: 'fresh' (skip cache lookup, write fresh; useful when callers know they want bypass), 'cache_only' (return cached hits only, no fresh resolution). Default unset — substrate uses per-resolver policy from the design's §3.3 matrix.

params.name: inline_skill_bodies
params.description: Chain 602. Per-request override of the TOOLKIT_PARSE_CONTEXT_INLINE_BODIES env var that gates skill-body inlining. When true (and a skill_trigger or discipline_skill ref resolves at single_exact), the envelope carries body_inlined / body_truncated / body_summary / body_bytes on the ref. Default unset → use env-var setting (default OFF during rollout). Pass false to force-disable for one call.

params.name: inline_budget_bytes
params.description: Chain 602. Overrides the default 20 KB envelope budget for inlined skill bodies. Zero or unset uses the default. The per-skill cap (8 KB) is not caller-tunable; raising the envelope budget is enough.

errors.message: Returned when the server started without the refresolve registry wired (degraded mode). Returns {error: 'registry not configured'} on the response envelope instead of a Go error.

examples.description: Basic call — orient on a shape-heavy user message.
examples.call: {"action":"parse_context","params":{"message_text":"please finish T8 and T9 in reference-resolution-substrate; T7 is blocked"}}

examples.description: Tighter budget when calling parse_context on a latency-sensitive path.
Force a fresh resolve, bypassing the session cache.

parse_context.notes:
Shape coverage:

  • chain_slug / task_slug / bug_slug → DB row in the corresponding table
  • path → filesystem under RepoRoot (file or directory)
  • skill_name → skills/<name>/ directories
  • tool_name → action-manifests/*.toml basenames
  • forge_schema → blueprints/forge-schemas/*.toml basenames
  • project_name → KnownProjects catalog
  • library_entry → library_index.slug or title
  • domain_term → vault_search via Qwen rubric classifier
  • external_technical → kiwix_search + (when wired) kiwix_bridge resolver
  • friction_shape → bug-filing suggestion (supersedes the friction-filing-reminder Stop hook for in-message detection)
  • skill_trigger → skills/_manifest.toml trigger keywords (T5 follow-on)
  • memory_entry → MEMORY.md index entries (T5 follow-on)
  • vault_candidate → vault_search bridge with domain-condition gate (T5 follow-on)
  • kiwix_bridge → kiwix_search bridge for external-technical terms (T5 follow-on)
  • discipline_skill → discipline-skill bodies whose trigger condition fires on the message (T5 follow-on)

Confidence tier semantics (see docs/REFERENCE_RESOLUTION.md §4):

  • single_exact → recommended_action=use_directly; PresentedAs is the canonical attribution string.
  • fuzzy_multi → recommended_action=ask_user_to_disambiguate; PresentedAs enumerates the top candidates.
  • weak_domain → recommended_action=mention_as_possibly_relevant; PresentedAs hedges with the top hit and score.
  • no_hit → recommended_action=acknowledge_no_hit_and_ask; PresentedAs says the token didn't resolve.

Each resolved reference carries a grounding_event_id. Every call emits one grounding_events row per detected reference with query_source='reference_resolution'.

Filter cache: per-session, keyed by (session_id, token, shape) with per-resolver policies (see docs/PARSE_CONTEXT.md §3.3 + §4). The cache_hits and cache_misses fields on the response envelope distinguish cached re-calls from fresh resolution. T5 follow-on phase wires the cache; the shell exposes the envelope fields with zero values until then.

`resolve_references` is a soft alias of `parse_context` — both dispatch into the same handler core, so existing callers continue to work without modification. New callers prefer parse_context.

History: shipped as a back-compat-preserving alias by chain reference-resolution-migration T5 (Phase 1). Follow-on phases: cache layer, then per-resolver shape additions (skill_trigger, memory_entry, vault_candidate, kiwix_bridge, discipline_skill).

record_query_interaction.purpose: Record one click-signal (a query_interactions row) against the grounding_events row of a prior search call, identified by that call's span_id. The owned write path for a client that detects click signals from its own transcript (the RAG implicit-feedback loop) — the grounding event is resolved server-side from span_id, so the client only needs the span id off its tool result plus the clicked hit's source_ref. A span with no grounding_event is a soft no-op.

params.name: span_id
params.description: The search call's substrate span id (off the tool result), used to resolve its grounding_event.

params.name: source_ref
params.description: The clicked/used hit's source ref (e.g. a vault note path).

params.name: click_kind
params.description: One of: followed | cited | mentioned | resolved-from.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The session the interaction occurred in.

params.name: position
params.description: 1-based rank of the clicked hit in the result list.

resolve_references.purpose: Soft alias of parse_context (the canonical orienting call). Resolves every reference-shape token in a user message in one round-trip — chain/task/bug slugs, paths, skill names, project names, tool names, forge schemas, library entries, domain terms, external-technical terms, friction shapes. Returns per-reference shape + confidence tier + recommended_action + PresentedAs. New callers should prefer parse_context (which adds skills/memory/vault/kiwix/discipline coverage and a filter cache); existing callers keep working unchanged.

params.name: message_text
params.description: The user message (or any agent-facing text) to scan for unresolved references. Empty string returns an empty References list and zero resolver calls.

params.name: top_k_per_shape
params.description: Cap on candidates each resolver returns after dispatcher post-processing. Defaults to 5.

params.name: include_no_hits
params.description: When true, references that resolved to no_hit stay in the References list (with recommended_action = acknowledge_no_hit_and_ask) instead of collapsing into the NoHitTokens slice. Default false.

params.name: total_budget_ms
params.description: Dispatcher wall-clock cap for the whole call, in milliseconds. Defaults to 2000 (2s). When the budget trips, truncated_by_budget=true is set on the response envelope; resolvers not reached surface ErrTotalBudgetExceeded as partial_failures.

errors.message: Returned when the server started without the refresolve registry wired (degraded mode). Returns {error: 'registry not configured'} on the response envelope instead of a Go error.

examples.description: Basic call — detect every shape in a mixed message.
examples.call: {"action":"resolve_references","params":{"message_text":"Working on reference-resolution-substrate and considering the Tripolar Invariant alongside docs/REFERENCE_RESOLUTION.md"}}

examples.description: Tighter budget for latency-sensitive contexts. Default budget is 2000ms — pass a smaller value when you want to fail fast on slow resolvers (vault rerank, kiwix index).
Keep no_hit references inline so the agent can acknowledge each one explicitly — useful when authoring a response that names everything the user mentioned.

resolve_references.notes:
Shape coverage and which catalog each shape resolves against:

  • chain_slug / task_slug / bug_slug → DB row in the corresponding table
  • path → filesystem under RepoRoot (file or directory)
  • skill_name → skills/*.toml basenames
  • tool_name → action-manifests/*.toml basenames
  • forge_schema → blueprints/forge-schemas/*.toml basenames
  • project_name → KnownProjects catalog
  • library_entry → library_index.slug (case-sensitive) OR title (case-insensitive)
  • domain_term → vault_search via Qwen rubric classifier (when configured)
  • external_technical → kiwix_search
  • friction_shape → a synthetic 'binding' suggesting a bug-filing call; supersedes the friction-filing-reminder Stop hook for in-message detection

Confidence tier semantics (see docs/REFERENCE_RESOLUTION.md §4):

  • single_exact → recommended_action=use_directly; PresentedAs is the canonical attribution string.
  • fuzzy_multi → recommended_action=ask_user_to_disambiguate; PresentedAs enumerates the top candidates.
  • weak_domain → recommended_action=mention_as_possibly_relevant; PresentedAs hedges with the top hit and score.
  • no_hit → recommended_action=acknowledge_no_hit_and_ask; PresentedAs says the token didn't resolve.

Each resolved reference also carries a grounding_event_id (T5). Every call emits one grounding_events row per detected reference with query_source='reference_resolution' so downstream telemetry (query_interactions, retrieval-success scoring) attributes the work to the resolver layer rather than the underlying per-shape tools.

The v1 reference-resolution skill (action-manifests/resolve-references.toml + .md) teaches the trigger discipline and presentation conventions; this MCP action is the mechanism the skill delegates to. Call this action directly when authoring an agent loop that needs reference binding without invoking the skill.

Default total_budget_ms is 2000. Pre-bug-1410, truncated_by_budget only fired on caller-supplied budgets; the handler now applies defaults locally so the flag fires on the default 2s ceiling too.

History: shipped by chain reference-resolution-substrate T4 (commit 8a88a0e); T5 added grounding-events emission; T6 added the friction_shape detector + supersession of the Stop-hook reminder. This action-docs chunk was backfilled when bug `resolve-references-missing-from-action-docs-corpus` flagged that the action was invisible to admin.action_describe despite being live in the dispatch table. Reference-resolution-migration T5 demoted it to a soft alias of parse_context; both dispatch into the same handler core.

vault_read.purpose: Return the note content + parsed frontmatter for one vault note. The path comes from vault_search results.

params.name: path
params.description: Vault note path, as returned by vault_search.

vault_search.purpose: Rank notes from ~/.claude/vault/ via local Qwen2.5-32B over the full path list. Call this when picking up a task in a domain you've worked in before, BEFORE assuming you remember the prior decisions; the agent's vault is the canonical store of cross-session decisions/learnings/reference.

params.name: query
params.description: Free-text search query.

params.name: top_k
params.description: Number of top results to return. Defaults to 5.
